data.title: CHWY Earnings: Chewy Stock Rockets as Customers Ramp Up Spending on Their Pets
data.description:
Shares of pet group Chewy (CHWY) bounded nearly 7% higher in pre-market trading after it said it had attracted thousands of new high-spending dog-and-cat loving customers.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The pet products e-commerce group said that adjusted EBITDA increased by 30.4% to $162.3 million during the quarter, with net sales of $3.26 billion up by 0.5% year over year. Basic earnings per share came in at $0.09, an increase of $0.03 year over year.

  • Chewy said that it recorded a net add in active customers of 813,000 in 2025, with net sales per customer of $591. This is up from $567 in 2024.
  • Its Autoship subscription-based service that lets customers schedule automatic deliveries of pet food, treats, and medication at regular intervals, also proved popular. It said Autoship customer sales climbed 12.8% year over year in Q4 and 14% over the full year.

Position of Real Strength

"We exited 2025 from a position of real strength: $12.6 billion in net sales, or 8.3% growth; $719 million of adjusted EBITDA, or 26% growth; record free cash flow of $562 million; and 21.3 million active customers," highlighted CEO Sumit Singh. "That performance underscores the durability of the Chewy model and gives us confidence in our ability to deliver continued profitable growth, expanding margins, and strong cash generation in 2026 and beyond."

Looking ahead, Chewy said it sees Q1 sales of $3.33 billion to $3.36 billion against consensus of $3.37 billion.

  • It forecasts Q1 EPS of $0.40 to $0.45 against $0.42 consensus.
  • For the full year, Chewy guided for sales of $13.6 billion to $13.75 billion versus a $13.59 billion consensus.

Is CHWY a Good Stock to Buy Now?

On TipRanks, CHWY has a Strong Buy consensus based on 11 Buy ratings. Its highest price target is $52. CHWY stock's consensus price target is $44.40, implying an 89.34% upside.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: ASML vs.  Applied Materials (AMAT): Which Chip Equipment Stock Offers Better Value in 2026?
data.description:
ASML Holding (ASML) and Applied Materials (AMAT) are two key players in the semiconductor equipment market, each providing chip manufacturers with very different types of manufacturing systems. Using the TipRanks Stocks Comparison Tool, we analyzed both companies to see which stock analysts favor. Both currently carry Strong Buy ratings, with roughly 18% upside potential. Valuation sets the two apart. ASML commands a premium thanks to its unique EUV technology and market dominance. By comparison, AMAT is more reasonably priced, with a diversified product lineup.

data.title: Why Is iTonic Holdings Stock (ITOC) Up Today?
data.description:
iTonic Holdings (ITOC) stock rocketed higher on Wednesday after the healthcare solutions provider with a focus on brachytherapy, the treatment of tumors with direct radiation, announced details of a private placement. A filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) revealed that the company has reached a private placement subscription agreement with certain investors.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

iTonic Holdings said that this private placement will include 100 million shares of its Class A stock. The shares will be sold for 20 cents each, which will bring the gross proceeds from the private placement to approximately $20 million.

iTonic Holdings noted that the private placement is expected to close in April 2026, after certain closing conditions are met. In addition to that, the shares included in this private placement are subject to a six-month lockup period, meaning that they can't be sold or traded directly or indirectly during the first six months after the private placement is finished.

iTonic Holdings Stock Movement Today

News of the private placement ignited interest in iTonic Holdings stock on Wednesday. As a result, the shares were up 92.39% in pre-market trading today. This follows a 2.82% dip yesterday. The stock has also fallen 32.12% year-to-date and 92.19% over the past 12 months.

With today's news came heavy trading of ITOC stock, as more than 32 million shares changed hands. To put that in perspective, the company's three-month daily average trading volume is well below that at about 110,000 shares.
data.author: William White

data.title: Pop Mart Stock Plunges as 'Concern on the Durability' of Labubu Grows
data.description:
Pop Mart International Group shares (HK:9992) crashed by a record 22.5% over the past five days. The massive selloff happened after the company's full-year results showed it is becoming way too dependent on its famous Labubu monster doll. Even though the company made a lot of money last year, investors are worried that Pop Mart cannot keep up this pace without finding another big hit.

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 3/25/26 – Stock Futures Rise on U.S.-Iran Peace Negotiations
data.description:
U.S. stock futures climbed early Wednesday after President Trump announced that the U.S. had sent a 15-point peace plan to Iran. Oil prices fell sharply due to rising hopes for de-escalation. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 Index (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were up 1.05%, 0.89%, and 0.93%, respectively, at 5:27 a.m. EST on March 25.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade QQQ with leverage
  • New trading tool for QQQ bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade QQQ

At the time of writing, the global oil benchmark Brent (CM:BZ) was down 1.01% at $98.92 per barrel, while West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude (CM:CL) fell 1.05% to $87.90 per barrel.

During Tuesday's regular trading, the three major indexes closed lower, reversing earlier gains from President Trump's claims of "productive conversations." Markets dipped after Iranian officials denied direct peace talks with the U.S. The Dow fell 0.18%, the Nasdaq declined 0.84%, and the S&P 500 shed 0.37%.

Notably, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield fell to near 4.33%. Additionally, the Spot Gold U.S. dollar price hovered around $4,565 per ounce on Wednesday.

No key economic reports are scheduled today. On the earnings front, companies reporting today include Beyond Meat (BYND), Chewy (CHWY), PDD Holdings (PDD), Paychex (PAYX), and Jefferies (JEF).

Elsewhere, European indexes opened higher today amid signs of U.S. efforts to de-escalate tensions.

Asia-Pacific Markets Trade Higher

Asia-Pacific markets advanced on March 25, as global investors reacted to easing U.S.-Iran tensions.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index gained 1.09%. In China, the Shanghai Composite rose 1.30%, while the Shenzhen Component surged 1.99%. Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei increased 2.87% and the Topix added 2.57%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: CoreWeave (CRWV) or Nebius (NBIS)? BofA's Top AI Infrastructure Pick
data.description:
Bank of America Securities' top analyst Tal Liani initiated coverage of AI infrastructure leaders CoreWeave (CRWV) and Nebius (NBIS). Both companies compete to power the explosive growth in generative AI workloads via infrastructure-as-a-service (IaaS) offerings. Liani issued Buy ratings for each, with a $100 price target for CRWV (20.5% upside from current levels) and $150 for NBIS (30.5% upside). Over the past year, CRWV shares have surged nearly 124% while NBIS has skyrocketed more than 322%.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade CRWV with leverage
  • New trading tool for CRWV bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade CRWV

Liani is a top analyst on TipRanks, ranking #1,058 out of 12,128 analysts tracked. He has a 55% success rate and an average return per rating of 8.30%.

Is CoreWeave a Good Investment?

CoreWeave, a cloud provider specializing in GPU-heavy data centers, is poised to capture share in the $79 billion AI IaaS market. Liani cited sustained demand for AI compute power, proprietary software tailored for AI workloads, and key partnerships with giants like Nvidia (NVDA) and OpenAI as key drivers.

"While risks exist, the demand trajectory remains solid," he noted, driven by Agentic AI's surging infrastructure needs. CoreWeave helps customers navigate supply, capacity, and power shortages, a gap unlikely to close before 2029. Liani values the stock at 21x CY27E EV/EBIT (enterprise value/earnings before interest and taxes), above the peer average of 16x.

Not all analysts share Liani's optimism. On TipRanks, CRWV has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on 13 Buys, nine Holds, and one Sell rating. The average CoreWeave price target of $112.81 implies 35.9% upside potential.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Microsoft ($MSFT): Does the Multiple Fully Reflect the AI and Cloud Flywheel?
data.description:
Microsoft (MSFT) is building a powerful artificial intelligence (AI) and cloud flywheel, but the current multiple does not fully reflect its long-term potential. While the company has emerged as a core AI infrastructure provider through Azure and Copilot, the stock has lagged some big tech peers as investors focus on rising capex and question the timing of monetization. However, strong fundamentals — including sustained cloud growth, early signs of AI monetization, and a robust balance sheet — support a bullish view that the opportunity remains underappreciated.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade AMZN with leverage
  • New trading tool for AMZN bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade AMZN

Azure's AI Momentum vs. Cloud Rivals

Azure is still at the heart of Microsoft's cloud story and its AI foray. Recent analysis indicates Azure's revenue has re-accelerated into the high-30% range year-over-year, with AI workloads playing a major role in its growth. Microsoft's multi-year exclusive partnership with OpenAI positions Azure effectively for global AI demand.

This makes Azure the leading choice for enterprises deploying OpenAI-based models, as it offers enterprise-grade features such as private networking, data residency, security, and compliance. It puts Azure in a league of its own in a market where basic compute and storage are becoming more commoditized.

Recent developments, however, have made this symbiotic monopoly a strategic hedge. While Azure remains the cornerstone for OpenAI's legacy Application Programming Interface (API) traffic, OpenAI's transition into an independent platform provider means Azure must now compete to earn OpenAI's future workloads. Azure's benefit has shifted from being the exclusive gateway to being the largest beneficiary of a massive, multi-cloud AI ecosystem — even as it competes directly with OpenAI for the same enterprise customers.

Copilot: Monetizing AI Buzz into Per-Seat Revenue

Copilot, another flagbearer of Microsoft's AI flywheel, turns AI from a feature into a price line item inside Microsoft 365. Its users grew more than 160% year-over-year to around 15 million in Q2 2026. For a company with about 450 million commercial Microsoft 365 users, a 3.3% penetration remains low, but Copilot is already generating billions of dollars in annual recurring revenue (ARR).

Typically, Copilot is sold as a premium add-on that lifts revenue per seat. Microsoft is now preparing a higher-tier E7 subscription priced at $99 per user per month, approximately 65% above its current top-tier enterprise plan. If the strategy is successful, Microsoft reconfigures what enterprises normally regard as a standard productivity-seat price in exchange for workflow automation, time savings, and integrated AI assistants.

High Switching Costs vs. Capex and Competition

Microsoft's long-established moat is high switching costs in software, infrastructure, and tools. Embedding Copilot across Word, Excel, Teams, PowerPoint, and Outlook enhances that competitive advantage. Once employees rely on AI-driven automation, the stress of switching from Microsoft 365 rises. This leads to stable pricing power, customer retention, and recurring revenue.

However, none of this is being handed over on a silver platter. Microsoft's AI capex has ramped aggressively, with some estimates suggesting recent quarterly capex has surpassed the company's entire annual capex in Fiscal 2023. Consequently, free cash flow has reduced. This feeds a narrative that Big Tech may be overspending on data centers without a definite payback timeline, thereby worrying investors.

Furthermore, Microsoft faces intense competition from Amazon (AMZN), Google (GOOGL), and other top AI infrastructure and software providers, which intensifies pressure on pricing and innovation. 

Still, Microsoft enjoys some leeway that many of its cloud or AI peers lack. First, its gross margin and operating margin remain high by large-cap standards. Second, the company's balance sheet gives it flexibility to front-load investments without affecting the dividend. Consequently, with the stock trading at a forward P/E of nearly 23.6x, the valuation appears increasingly grounded compared to historical highs. Lastly, the OpenAI partnership reduces risk for Microsoft by successfully pre-booking AI demand for Azure.

What Is the Market's View?

TipRanks' data shows that MSFT has a Strong Buy consensus rating. Based on 36 Wall Street analysts' recommendations over the past three months, there are 33 Buys, three Holds, and zero Sells. Microsoft also has a high Smart Score of 8, supported by positive institutional sentiment and solid hedge fund interest.

The average 12-month price target is around $583.68, with a wide range from $392 to $678. The average price target denotes a 56.59% upside from the previous price of $372.74. TipRanks' market view supports the bullish case for Microsoft, as the quantitative picture aligns with the qualitative chance for additional gains.
data.author: Tobi Opeyemi Amure
